title: Velvety Crispy Argentinian Flaxseed Fritters
description: Al dente, crispy fritters infused with Argentinian flavors, using flaxseed meal for a velvety bite and chickpea flour for a hearty texture.
instructions: In a bowl whisk together flaxseed meal, chickpea flour, salt, pepper, cumin, oregano, and parsley until well mixed.
Add water gradually, stirring until a thick batter forms with no lumps.
Let the batter rest for 10 minutes to allow the flaxseed to swell and thicken.
Heat a non‑stick skillet over medium heat and add olive oil, swirling to coat the bottom.
Once the oil reaches 375°F, ladle spoonfuls of batter into the skillet, spacing them about 2 inches apart.
Sear the fritters for 3 minutes on one side until golden and crisp, then flip carefully.
Cook the other side for an additional 2–3 minutes until evenly browned.
Remove the fritters onto a paper towel‑lined plate to drain excess oil.
Season the hot fritters with a pinch more salt if desired.
Serve immediately with a squeeze of fresh lemon or a side of chimichurri sauce.
